title: Burns Paiute Tribal Code
covered_parties: Users of the Burns Paiute Tribe website seeking published Tribal Code documents and resolutions.
covered_data: Index metadata and links for Tribal Code documents and related resolutions; the fetched bytes do not contain the linked instruments' substantive provisions.

requirements.none_reason: The fetched document is an official code index page, not the text of an enacted instrument, and states no partner-facing data-governance requirement.

tsdf_mapping.notes: An index of linked legal materials does not itself establish a TSDF-relevant sharing rule.

notes: DRAFT machine extraction; no Nation-attributed claim is confirmed by the Nation.

title: Research Regulation (Tribal Code Chapter 1-10)
issuing_entity: Confederated Tribes of Coos, Lower Umpqua and Siuslaw Indians
covered_parties: Individuals, corporations, agencies, and public or private institutions seeking to conduct studies, surveys, or research projects on the Tribes' lands when the work was not specifically requested or contracted for by the Tribes; Tribal employees also have a records-nondisclosure duty.
covered_data: Study, survey, and research designs, funding and personnel information, source information, results and products, intended publications, Tribal files and records, and interim, progress, and final reports concerning projects on the Tribes' lands.

claims.claim: Any individual, corporation, agency, or public or private institution wishing to undertake a study, survey, or research project on the Tribes' lands must first obtain a permit approved by the Tribal Council or its designate.
claims.conditions: Applies when the project was not specifically requested or contracted for by the Tribes.

claims.claim: Before a permit may issue, the applicant must execute a written agreement with the Tribes governing how the project will be conducted and creating a fiduciary relationship.
claims.conditions: Applies when the project was not specifically requested or contracted for by the Tribes.

claims.claim: The required written agreement must limit the researcher's right to publish results or conclusions until the Tribes have an opportunity to review and comment, and the final published product must include comments officially made by or for the Tribes.
claims.conditions: Applies to the agreement required for a project not specifically requested or contracted for by the Tribes; the provision requires review and inclusion of comments, not express Tribal approval of publication.

claims.claim: Before a permit may issue, the applicant must provide for approval the project's purpose and profit status, funding source and amount, methodology, participants, dates, information sources, intended final product and publication plan, intended use of results, and steps protecting Tribal and member rights.
claims.conditions: Approval is by the Tribal Council chair or designate; a performance bond is additionally required only when the Tribes deem it appropriate.

claims.claim: The Tribes may cancel a permit if the project is or has been conducted contrary to the approved study design or the agreement supporting the permit.
claims.conditions: Cancellation is conditioned on an apparent deviation from the approved design or underlying agreement.

claims.claim: The Tribes may refuse a permit when they determine that there is insufficient time for appropriate Tribal review, evaluation, supervision, or participation.
claims.conditions: Applies when the Tribes deem review time insufficient.

claims.claim: The Tribes may charge a permit fee when the project is conducted for profit or its results or publication are intended or likely to financially benefit the researcher or research organization.
claims.conditions: Limited to for-profit projects or projects whose results or publication are intended or likely to produce financial benefit.

claims.claim: The researcher must furnish the Tribal Chair's office, without charge, at least one copy of every interim or progress report and the final report resulting from the project.
claims.conditions: Applies to reports resulting from a study, survey, or research project governed by the chapter.

claims.claim: Tribal employees must not provide information from Tribal files or records to a person or organization conducting research that is not authorized under the chapter.
claims.conditions: The nondisclosure duty applies when the recipient's research is not authorized under Chapter 1-10; violation exposes the employee to censure or dismissal.

claims.claim: A person attempting research that was neither specifically requested or contracted for by the Tribes nor permitted under the chapter is subject to available civil remedies under Tribal and Oregon law.
claims.conditions: Applies only to research outside both stated authorization paths.

tsdf_mapping.notes: The permit-and-written-agreement gate partly resembles T2 negotiated sharing, and the unauthorized-records restriction resembles sovereign control; publication-comment rights, fiduciary duties, fees, report return, and remedies are not represented by tier behavior alone.

notes: DRAFT machine extraction; no Nation-attributed claim is confirmed by the Nation. Current-status assessment is based on the official published code chapter and its legislative-history statement.

title: Research Ordinance, Siletz Tribal Code § 9.100
covered_parties: All persons subject to the Tribe's civil jurisdiction who undertake research directly affecting the Tribe or Tribal members, and all researchers conducting projects on Tribal lands or directly affecting the Tribe, Tribal lands, or Tribal members, including requested, sponsored, independent, Tribal, state, and federal researchers; defined minor projects receive limited exceptions.
covered_data: Research project proposals, applications, contracts, permits, progress and result reports, all project data and work product, documents, correspondence, specimens, raw materials, Tribal Intellectual Property, and information affecting the Tribe, Tribal lands, or Tribal members.

claims.claim: A person undertaking research directly affecting the Tribe or Tribal members is deemed to have consented to the legislative and regulatory jurisdiction of the Tribal Council and the adjudicatory jurisdiction of the Tribal Court.
claims.conditions: Applies to research activities directly affecting the Tribe or Tribal members and is bounded by the ordinance's territorial and civil-jurisdiction scope.

claims.claim: A researcher must obtain a Tribal Council-approved permit before undertaking a project on Tribal lands or directly affecting the Tribe, Tribal lands, or Tribal members.
claims.conditions: Extends to research requested or sponsored by Tribal bodies and to independent and Tribal, state, or federal researchers, subject to the minor-project exception in § 9.104.

claims.claim: A minor-project researcher must complete a Research Permit application if the project later ceases to qualify as minor.
claims.conditions: The initial exemption applies only when the project will not invade Tribal members' privacy and will not be published or made publicly available.

claims.claim: A minor-project researcher must execute a waiver when the Tribe's chief executive determines that a waiver is necessary.
claims.conditions: The researcher may proceed before the executed waiver is received only after orally agreeing to execute it.

claims.claim: By executing the minor-project waiver, the researcher agrees not to invade Tribal members' privacy and not to publish or make the project publicly available without the Tribe's written permission.
claims.conditions: Applies to a minor project for which the waiver is required; a student must attach the waiver to submitted work or otherwise prevent publication without the student's knowledge or Tribal permission.

claims.claim: The Tribe may require the researcher to pay or contribute to fees and expenses for an outside consultant needed to review a research proposal.
claims.conditions: Applies when the research clearinghouse needs outside expertise and the Tribe must hire a consultant.

claims.claim: A prospective researcher must complete the Tribe's application materials and appear at the Tribal Council meeting at which the proposal and clearinghouse recommendations are presented.
claims.conditions: Applies to projects that require a research permit under the ordinance.

claims.claim: Researchers must provide for Tribal oversight and report regularly to the Tribal Council and liaison department on project progress and results.
claims.conditions: Applies to permitted research projects governed by the ordinance.

claims.claim: The Tribe may retain sole ownership of all data, documents, correspondence, reports, specimens, and other information or items produced, generated, or gathered by the researcher.
claims.conditions: The Research Contract may make an express exception to sole Tribal ownership.

claims.claim: The Tribe may withdraw consent to use or release information and may prevent publication of data that is unauthorized, insensitive, misrepresents or stereotypes Tribal people, or will harm the Tribe, its health, safety, welfare, or environment.
claims.conditions: Exercise is limited to the stated unauthorized, insensitive, misrepresentative, stereotyping, or harmful uses or publications.

claims.claim: If the Tribe withdraws approval of a research project, the researcher must return all information, data, raw materials, and specimens to the Tribe.
claims.conditions: Triggered only when the Tribe withdraws project approval.

claims.claim: A researcher must not use work product generated under a Research Contract or Permit for another purpose without the Tribal Council's express written consent.
claims.conditions: Applies to secondary use of work product generated under the Research Contract or Permit.

claims.claim: For violations, the Tribal Court may grant injunctive relief, order preservation and return of property including data, forfeit a performance bond, order restitution, impose civil penalties up to $5,000, and grant other relief needed to redress injury.
claims.conditions: Available in an action brought for violation of the ordinance; separate provisions also authorize removal, civil suit, and affiliation consequences.

tsdf_mapping.notes: Permit and written-consent gates partly resemble T2 negotiation and T3 sovereign control. The ordinance goes materially beyond TSDF tiers through jurisdiction, ownership, privacy and publication controls, return duties, secondary-use consent, and judicial remedies.

notes: DRAFT machine extraction; no Nation-attributed claim is confirmed by the Nation. Modal and exception language follows the version amended through 2025-02-20.

title: Coquille Indian Tribal Code Chapter 146: Indigenous Determinants of Health Ordinance
covered_parties: The Coquille Indian Tribe and Tribal Council in defining, approving, reviewing, and monitoring Indigenous Determinants of Health and related policy.
covered_data: Health-related data, needs, and metrics used to identify and evaluate Indigenous Determinants of Health, including social, political, environmental, cultural, spiritual, and economic conditions.

requirements.none_reason: The ordinance establishes an internal Tribal framework for Indigenous Determinants of Health and Tribal data-sovereignty metrics but imposes no concrete partner-facing data-sharing obligation.

tsdf_mapping.notes: The ordinance expressly reserves Tribal determination of Indigenous Determinants of Health and calls for data sovereignty in metrics, conceptually aligning with sovereign control, but it specifies no TSDF tier, egress rule, agreement term, or external-partner duty.

notes: DRAFT machine extraction; no Nation-attributed claim is confirmed by the Nation. The official Tribal Code chapter presents itself as an ordinance, but the bytes do not state an adoption date.

title: Chapter 110 Freedom of Information Ordinance
issuing_entity: Confederated Tribes of the Grand Ronde Community of Oregon
covered_parties: Tribal Administration, the Records Administrator, Tribal members, Tribal newspaper staff acting officially, Tribal officials and employees, and any person who possesses or distributes a Tribal record or information from one.
covered_data: Records made, received, filed, or recorded in connection with Tribal business, subject to stated exclusions and including confidential, personal, audit, security, land, privileged, strategy, investment, and mixed exempt/nonexempt records.

claims.claim: Tribal Administration must adopt policies for retaining, managing, and destroying records in its custody and must comply with applicable federal and Oregon records-retention requirements.
claims.conditions: Policy development must consider records' cultural, historical, legal, administrative, and research value.

claims.claim: Only Tribal members and Tribal newspaper staff acting in their official capacity have a right under the ordinance to obtain copies of covered records.
claims.conditions: The right remains subject to the ordinance's unavailable-record categories, request procedures, and department-policy limits.

claims.claim: Tribal newspaper staff may use records obtained under the ordinance only in connection with their official newspaper work.
claims.conditions: Applies to copies obtained by newspaper staff under the ordinance.

claims.claim: Records in the ordinance's unavailable categories must not be released to the membership except through the category-specific exceptions stated by the ordinance.
claims.conditions: Categories include records confidential under law or policy; investigatory audits; security reports; land transactions; confidentiality restrictions or court orders; privileged or work-product material; records whose disclosure may adversely affect Tribal political or business strategies; and investment directives, strategies, and holdings. Some categories permit release by the identified Tribal authority, and personal records may be available to the member they identify.

claims.claim: When a record contains both available and exempt information, the Records Administrator must separate the information and provide the nonexempt portion through redaction or another method that makes the withholding apparent.
claims.conditions: Applies only to mixed records containing both information available under the ordinance and information exempt from disclosure.

claims.claim: Supplemental information submitted by a Tribal member to address allegedly false or misleading information must be kept with the applicable record.
claims.conditions: The member must be identified in the record and submit the required written summary and additional information to the appropriate Tribal offices.

claims.claim: A Tribal official or employee must not provide a record or information from it to a person who is not authorized to have access under the ordinance.
claims.conditions: Disclosure is permitted when it is in the ordinary course of the official's or employee's position or is authorized by Tribal Council.

claims.claim: A person must not knowingly possess or distribute a record, or information contained in a record, that the person is not authorized to possess or distribute.
claims.conditions: The violation requires knowing possession or distribution without authorization.

claims.claim: A person found by the Tribal Court to have violated the ordinance is subject to escalating civil fines for the first three violations and loss of the right to view or obtain record copies after a fourth violation.
claims.conditions: Sanctions depend on a Tribal Court finding and the number of prior violations.

tsdf_mapping.notes: The ordinance uses membership eligibility, record-specific exemptions, redaction, purpose limitation, and sanctions rather than TSDF tiers. Treating membership access as T1 or every exempt record as T3 would flatten materially different legal conditions.

sensitivity_flags.notes: Institution-only attribution applied; no individual names or email addresses are present in this record.

notes: DRAFT machine extraction; no Nation-attributed claim is confirmed by the Nation. Owner handling rule applied throughout.

title: Temporary Special-Use Permit with Attached Memorandum of Agreement
issuing_entity: The Klamath Tribes and United States Department of Agriculture, Forest Service
covered_parties: The Klamath Tribes as permit holder and the United States Forest Service as permitting agency and government-to-government consultation partner.
covered_data: Operating and maintenance plans, management and resource information, scientific data and analysis, Tribal input and recommendations, project information, consultation materials, and meeting minutes exchanged for resource-management decisions.

claims.claim: The permit holder must provide an operating plan prepared in consultation with the authorized agency officer, and plan revisions must be submitted by the holder and approved by the agency.
claims.conditions: The plan covers facility operations and maintenance, operating dates or season, and other information the authorized officer requires to manage and evaluate the authorized use; the approved plan and annual revisions become part of the authorization.

claims.claim: The permit states that federal privacy and freedom-of-information law governs the confidentiality afforded to information received by the Forest Service.
claims.conditions: Applies to information collected by the Forest Service to evaluate and administer the authorization.

claims.claim: The parties' staff must meet or exchange information as needed during planning and decision-making and discuss the decision process, scientific data, information and analysis, and Tribal input and recommendations; Tribal input must be treated as baseline data for the action.
claims.conditions: Applies throughout the planning and decision-making process under the attached government-to-government agreement.

claims.claim: The attached agreement identifies maintaining confidentiality for cultural resources as an issue to be addressed through consultation.
claims.conditions: Applies within the agreement's consultation over resource use and protection.
claims.cite: Attached MOA, Intent and Objectives ¶(g) (PDF p. 12)

claims.claim: Before public scoping of a proposed project or activity, the parties must share all relevant information, and designated Tribal staff input must be considered baseline data for developing the activity or policy.
claims.conditions: Applies to the attached agreement's pre-scoping consultation process; timing and emergency exceptions remain governed by that process.
claims.cite: Attached MOA, Tribal Involvement Prior to Public Scoping ¶¶1-2 (PDF p. 15)

claims.claim: Before public release of an environmental assessment or draft environmental impact statement, the parties must discuss relevant information and make every effort to communicate fully, share information, and seek mutually beneficial resolutions.
claims.conditions: Applies to consultation before the specified public release and remains subject to the agreement's dispute-resolution procedure if the parties cannot resolve differences.
claims.cite: Attached MOA, Consultation Prior to Release of EA or Draft EIS ¶2 (PDF pp. 15-16)

claims.claim: Minutes of the parties' quarterly resource-consultation meetings must be kept and copies sent to the Klamath Tribes.
claims.conditions: Applies to the quarterly meetings established by the attached agreement.
claims.cite: Attached MOA, quarterly meetings provision (PDF p. 15)

tsdf_mapping.notes: The executed government-to-government information exchange resembles negotiated T2 sharing, while cultural-resource confidentiality resembles sovereign restriction. TSDF does not represent consultation timing, baseline-data treatment, meeting records, or external-law disclosure controls.

notes: DRAFT machine extraction; no Nation-attributed claim is confirmed by the Nation. Human review is required because the catalog enum has no precise status for the document's combination of a time-limited permit and an attached amended agreement. Owner handling rule applied; this record is limited to authorized resource-information and data-governance clauses.

covered_data: Index metadata and links for Tribal Court forms, ordinances, codes, regulations, and Law and Order Code articles; the fetched bytes do not contain the linked instruments' substantive provisions.

requirements.none_reason: The fetched document is an official code-and-forms index page, not an enacted code article, and states no partner-facing data-governance requirement.

title: Archives & Records
issuing_entity: Confederated Tribes of the Colville Reservation
covered_parties: Research Permit applicants and the Tribal administrative and council bodies that review their applications.
covered_data: Research Permit application documents and the research-project information submitted for administrative and Tribal council review.

claims.claim: A research applicant must submit the documents listed by the Archives & Records program to make a Research Permit application.
claims.conditions: The page supplies links to the application, ordinance process, research-regulation chapter, and related resolutions; this webpage is procedural guidance rather than the linked enacted instruments.
claims.cite: Archives & Records page, Research Permit Application introduction

claims.claim: A completed Research Permit application undergoes an administrative review and then review by a Tribal council committee for approval or disapproval, with the committee selected according to the research content.
claims.conditions: The page describes approximately 45 days for administrative review and advises allowing at least four additional weeks for council review.

claims.claim: Applicants should account for the stated review periods when selecting the proposed start date for a research project.
claims.conditions: The recommendation is tied to the page's approximate administrative-review and minimum council-review timeframes.

tsdf_mapping.notes: The sovereign permit-approval gate resembles negotiated partner access, but the landing page states review sequence and timing rather than TSDF classification, agreement content, or egress rules.

sensitivity_flags.notes: Institution-only attribution applied; no individual names or email addresses are present in this record.

notes: DRAFT machine extraction; no Nation-attributed claim is confirmed by the Nation. This record catalogs only the landing-page guidance, not the separately linked instruments.

title: Archives and Collections
covered_parties: Qualified researchers seeking access to the Spokane Tribe's archives and collections, and the Tribal institutions responsible for research clearance.
covered_data: Tribal archival and collection materials, including historic photographs, traditional stories, material culture, artifacts, and digitally presented archive content.

claims.claim: The Spokane Tribe's collections are available to qualified researchers only after research clearance has been granted through the Tribal Business Council and the Tribal Historic Preservation Officer.
claims.conditions: Applies to researchers seeking access to the collections described on the page; the page also points to a collections-access and use-agreement form but does not reproduce its terms.
claims.cite: Archives and Collections page, opening paragraph

tsdf_mapping.notes: Institutional clearance before qualified-researcher access resembles negotiated T2 partner sharing, but the page does not state a tier, the use agreement's terms, or egress controls.

sensitivity_flags.notes: Institution-only attribution applied; no individual names or email addresses are present in this record.

notes: DRAFT machine extraction; no Nation-attributed claim is confirmed by the Nation. Owner handling rule applied throughout.

title: Preservation Program
covered_parties: Members of the public encountering archaeological or cultural resources, qualified professionals seeking to affect their context, and federal, public, and private organizations receiving Tribal preservation services.
covered_data: Cultural heritage, archaeological sites, traditional cultural properties, cultural materials, historic records, intellectual property, artifacts, historic structures, and burial-related cultural resources.

claims.claim: Only qualified professionals with proper permits may affect the context of archaeological or cultural resources.
claims.conditions: The page presents this as a consequence of federal archaeological-resources law and Washington state law, not as a separately enacted Spokane Tribal rule.
claims.cite: Preservation Program page, 'The Laws That Preserve Our Past'

claims.claim: Visitors should enjoy cultural heritage without touching it, remove their trash, and report potential cultural finds to an appropriate historic-preservation institution.
claims.conditions: These are public-facing actions listed under the page's 'What You Can Do to Help' guidance.
claims.cite: Preservation Program page, 'What You Can Do to Help — Do'

claims.claim: Visitors should not collect or purchase artifacts, enter or dig in unauthorized areas, or deface, move, paint, or alter material that may be archaeological or historic.
claims.conditions: These are public-facing prohibitions listed as guidance; separate federal and state law may independently make particular conduct unlawful.
claims.cite: Preservation Program page, 'What You Can Do to Help — Don't'

tsdf_mapping.notes: The page concerns stewardship, physical cultural-resource disturbance, professional permits, and public conduct. TSDF data tiers do not represent those place- and object-based legal protections, even though the program mission also includes historic records and intellectual property.

notes: DRAFT machine extraction; no Nation-attributed claim is confirmed by the Nation. Legal statements are cataloged as the webpage's summary of external law, not as a claim that the webpage itself is enacted law.
